1вҶ’# mise: Python Ecosystem
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’Python version management, virtual environment handling, and common task patterns for Python projects.
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’## Python Version Management
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’mise manages Python versions through its core plugin. No separate plugin install required.
     8вҶ’
     9вҶ’```toml
    10вҶ’[tools]
    11вҶ’python = "3.12"               # Latest 3.12.x (prefix match)
    12вҶ’```
    13вҶ’
    14вҶ’Other version formats:
    15вҶ’
    16вҶ’```toml
    17вҶ’[tools]
    18вҶ’python = "3.12.4"             # Exact version
    19вҶ’# or
    20вҶ’python = ["3.11", "3.12"]     # Multiple versions (first is default)
    21вҶ’```
    22вҶ’
    23вҶ’By default, mise installs precompiled Python binaries for speed. To compile from source (needed for custom build flags or uncommon platforms):
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’```toml
    26вҶ’[settings]
    27вҶ’python.compile = true
    28вҶ’```
    29вҶ’
    30вҶ’### Idiomatic version files
    31вҶ’
    32вҶ’mise reads `.python-version` files when idiomatic version file support is enabled, providing compatibility with pyenv-based workflows.
    33вҶ’
    34вҶ’## Virtual Environments
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’Configure automatic venv creation and activation through the `[env]` section:
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’```toml
    39вҶ’[env]
    40вҶ’_.python.venv = { path = ".venv", create = true }
    41вҶ’```
    42вҶ’
    43вҶ’This creates `.venv` if it doesn't exist and activates it when entering the project directory.
    44вҶ’
    45вҶ’### Extended options
    46вҶ’
    47вҶ’```toml
    48вҶ’[env]
    49вҶ’_.python.venv = {
    50вҶ’  path = ".venv",
    51вҶ’  create = true,
    52вҶ’  python = "3.11",                          # Pin venv to specific version
    53вҶ’  uv_create_args = ["--seed"]               # Pass args to uv venv
    54вҶ’}
    55вҶ’```
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’### UV integration
    58вҶ’
    59вҶ’To align UV's Python resolution with the mise-managed version:
    60вҶ’
    61вҶ’```toml
    62вҶ’[env]
    63вҶ’UV_PYTHON = { value = "{{ tools.python.path }}", tools = true }
    64вҶ’```
    65вҶ’
    66вҶ’### Settings
    67вҶ’
    68вҶ’```toml
    69вҶ’[settings]
    70вҶ’python.uv_venv_auto = false                # false | "source" | "create|source" | true
    71вҶ’python.venv_stdlib = false                  # Use stdlib venv instead of uv/virtualenv
    72вҶ’```
    73вҶ’
    74вҶ’## uv Integration
    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’Most Python projects use uv as the package manager. Prefix commands with `uv run` to execute through the managed venv:
    77вҶ’
    78вҶ’```toml
    79вҶ’[tasks.python-install]
    80вҶ’description = "Install Python dependencies"
    81вҶ’run = "uv sync"
    82вҶ’
    83вҶ’[tasks.python-upgrade]
    84вҶ’description = "Upgrade Python dependencies"
    85вҶ’run = "uv lock --upgrade"
    86вҶ’
    87вҶ’[tasks.python-clean]
    88вҶ’description = "Clean development environment"
    89вҶ’run = ["rm -rf .venv", "rm -rf requirements.lock"]
    90вҶ’```
    91вҶ’
    92вҶ’For CI, use `uv sync --frozen` to install from the lockfile without updating it.
    93вҶ’
    94вҶ’## Task Patterns
    95вҶ’
    96вҶ’All task commands below assume uv-managed projects. Prefix with `uv run` when executing tools installed in the project venv.
    97вҶ’
    98вҶ’### Testing
    99вҶ’
   100вҶ’```toml
   101вҶ’[tasks.test]
   102вҶ’description = "Run test suite"
   103вҶ’run = "uv run pytest"
   104вҶ’
   105вҶ’[tasks."test:coverage"]
   106вҶ’description = "Run tests with coverage"
   107вҶ’run = "uv run pytest --cov=src --cov-report=term-missing"
   108вҶ’
   109вҶ’[tasks."test:watch"]
   110вҶ’description = "Run tests on file change"
   111вҶ’run = "uv run ptw -- -x"
   112вҶ’```
   113вҶ’
   114вҶ’### Linting and Formatting
   115вҶ’
   116вҶ’```toml
   117вҶ’[tasks.lint]
   118вҶ’description = "Check code style"
   119вҶ’run = "uv run ruff check ."
   120вҶ’
   121вҶ’[tasks."lint:fix"]
   122вҶ’description = "Auto-fix lint issues"
   123вҶ’run = "uv run ruff check --fix ."
   124вҶ’
   125вҶ’[tasks.format]
   126вҶ’description = "Format code"
   127вҶ’run = "uv run ruff format ."
   128вҶ’
   129вҶ’[tasks."format:check"]
   130вҶ’description = "Check formatting without changes"
   131вҶ’run = "uv run ruff format --check ."
   132вҶ’```
   133вҶ’
   134вҶ’### Type Checking
   135вҶ’
   136вҶ’```toml
   137вҶ’[tasks.typecheck]
   138вҶ’description = "Run type checker"
   139вҶ’run = "uv run mypy src/"
   140вҶ’```
   141вҶ’
   142вҶ’### Quality Gate
   143вҶ’
   144вҶ’```toml
   145вҶ’[tasks.check]
   146вҶ’description = "Full quality gate"
   147вҶ’depends = ["format:check", "lint", "typecheck", "test"]
   148вҶ’```
   149вҶ’
   150вҶ’### Packaging
   151вҶ’
   152вҶ’```toml
   153вҶ’[tasks.build]
   154вҶ’description = "Build distribution packages"
   155вҶ’depends = ["check"]
   156вҶ’run = "uv run python -m build"
   157вҶ’
   158вҶ’[tasks.publish]
   159вҶ’description = "Publish to PyPI"
   160вҶ’depends = ["build"]
   161вҶ’run = "uv run twine upload dist/*"
   162вҶ’```
   163вҶ’
   164вҶ’### Development Server
   165вҶ’
   166вҶ’```toml
   167вҶ’[tasks.serve]
   168вҶ’description = "Run development server"
   169вҶ’run = "uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000"
   170вҶ’
   171вҶ’[tasks."db:migrate"]
   172вҶ’description = "Run database migrations"
   173вҶ’run = "uv run alembic upgrade head"
   174вҶ’
   175вҶ’[tasks."db:revision"]
   176вҶ’description = "Create new migration"
   177вҶ’usage = 'arg "<message>" help="Migration description"'
   178вҶ’run = "uv run alembic revision --autogenerate -m \"$1\""
   179вҶ’```
   180вҶ’
   181вҶ’## Default Packages
   182вҶ’
   183вҶ’Create `~/.default-python-packages` with one package per line to auto-install into every new Python version:
   184вҶ’
   185вҶ’```text
   186вҶ’pip
   187вҶ’setuptools
   188вҶ’wheel
   189вҶ’pipx
   190вҶ’```
   191вҶ’
   192вҶ’Set `MISE_PYTHON_DEFAULT_PACKAGES_FILE` to use a custom path.
   193вҶ’

1вҶ’# mise: Rust Ecosystem
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’Rust toolchain management and common task patterns for Rust projects.
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’## Rust Toolchain Management
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’mise manages Rust through rustup. Installations live in rustup's directory structure, not mise's. mise sets `RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN` to activate the correct version.
     8вҶ’
     9вҶ’```toml
    10вҶ’[tools]
    11вҶ’rust = "stable"               # Most common
    12вҶ’```
    13вҶ’
    14вҶ’Other channel options:
    15вҶ’
    16вҶ’```toml
    17вҶ’[tools]
    18вҶ’rust = "1.83"                 # Pinned version
    19вҶ’# or
    20вҶ’rust = "nightly"              # Nightly channel
    21вҶ’```
    22вҶ’
    23вҶ’### Components and profiles
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’```toml
    26вҶ’[tools]
    27вҶ’rust = {
    28вҶ’  version = "stable",
    29вҶ’  components = "clippy,rustfmt,rust-src",
    30вҶ’  profile = "default"
    31вҶ’}
    32вҶ’```
    33вҶ’
    34вҶ’Profile options: `minimal` (compiler only), `default` (plus docs, rustfmt, clippy), `complete` (all components).
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’### Cross-compilation targets
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’```toml
    39вҶ’[tools]
    40вҶ’rust = {
    41вҶ’  version = "stable",
    42вҶ’  targets = "wasm32-unknown-unknown,aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu"
    43вҶ’}
    44вҶ’```
    45вҶ’
    46вҶ’### When to use mise vs rustup directly
    47вҶ’
    48вҶ’Use mise for version pinning in `mise.toml` вҖ” the project declares which Rust version it needs, and mise activates it. Rustup handles the underlying installation, component management, and target configuration. The two work together: mise selects, rustup provides.
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’### Rust settings
    51вҶ’
    52вҶ’```toml
    53вҶ’[settings]
    54вҶ’rust.cargo_home = "~/.cargo"
    55вҶ’rust.rustup_home = "~/.rustup"
    56вҶ’```
    57вҶ’
    58вҶ’These isolate mise's Rust installation from other rustup setups on the system when needed.
    59вҶ’
    60вҶ’## Task Patterns
    61вҶ’
    62вҶ’### Building
    63вҶ’
    64вҶ’```toml
    65вҶ’[tasks.build]
    66вҶ’description = "Build in debug mode"
    67вҶ’run = "cargo build"
    68вҶ’
    69вҶ’[tasks."build:release"]
    70вҶ’description = "Build optimized release binary"
    71вҶ’run = "cargo build --release"
    72вҶ’sources = ["src/**/*.rs", "Cargo.toml", "Cargo.lock"]
    73вҶ’outputs = ["target/release/{{env.CARGO_BIN_NAME}}"]
    74вҶ’```
    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’### Testing
    77вҶ’
    78вҶ’```toml
    79вҶ’[tasks.test]
    80вҶ’description = "Run test suite"
    81вҶ’run = "cargo test"
    82вҶ’
    83вҶ’[tasks."test:verbose"]
    84вҶ’description = "Run tests with output"
    85вҶ’run = "cargo test -- --nocapture"
    86вҶ’
    87вҶ’[tasks."test:doc"]
    88вҶ’description = "Run documentation tests"
    89вҶ’run = "cargo test --doc"
    90вҶ’```
    91вҶ’
    92вҶ’### Linting and Formatting
    93вҶ’
    94вҶ’```toml
    95вҶ’[tasks.lint]
    96вҶ’description = "Run clippy with strict warnings"
    97вҶ’run = "cargo clippy -- -D warnings"
    98вҶ’
    99вҶ’[tasks.format]
   100вҶ’description = "Format code"
   101вҶ’run = "cargo fmt"
   102вҶ’
   103вҶ’[tasks."format:check"]
   104вҶ’description = "Check formatting without changes"
   105вҶ’run = "cargo fmt -- --check"
   106вҶ’```
   107вҶ’
   108вҶ’### Quality Gate
   109вҶ’
   110вҶ’```toml
   111вҶ’[tasks.check]
   112вҶ’description = "Full quality gate"
   113вҶ’depends = ["format:check", "lint", "test"]
   114вҶ’```
   115вҶ’
   116вҶ’### Documentation
   117вҶ’
   118вҶ’```toml
   119вҶ’[tasks.doc]
   120вҶ’description = "Build and open docs"
   121вҶ’run = "cargo doc --open --no-deps"
   122вҶ’
   123вҶ’[tasks."doc:check"]
   124вҶ’description = "Check doc warnings"
   125вҶ’run = "RUSTDOCFLAGS='-D warnings' cargo doc --no-deps"
   126вҶ’```
   127вҶ’
   128вҶ’### Development Workflow
   129вҶ’
   130вҶ’```toml
   131вҶ’[tasks.watch]
   132вҶ’description = "Rebuild on file changes"
   133вҶ’run = "cargo watch -x build"
   134вҶ’
   135вҶ’[tasks.run]
   136вҶ’description = "Run the binary"
   137вҶ’depends = ["build"]
   138вҶ’run = "cargo run"
   139вҶ’
   140вҶ’[tasks.install]
   141вҶ’description = "Install binary locally"
   142вҶ’run = "cargo install --path ."
   143вҶ’```
   144вҶ’
   145вҶ’### Workspace Tasks
   146вҶ’
   147вҶ’For Cargo workspaces with multiple crates:
   148вҶ’
   149вҶ’```toml
   150вҶ’[tasks."ws:test"]
   151вҶ’description = "Test all workspace members"
   152вҶ’run = "cargo test --workspace"
   153вҶ’
   154вҶ’[tasks."ws:check"]
   155вҶ’description = "Check all workspace members"
   156вҶ’run = "cargo check --workspace"
   157вҶ’```
   158вҶ’
   159вҶ’## Cross-Compilation
   160вҶ’
   161вҶ’Set target-specific environment variables through `[env]`:
   162вҶ’
   163вҶ’```toml
   164вҶ’[env]
   165вҶ’CARGO_TARGET_AARCH64_UNKNOWN_LINUX_GNU_LINKER = "aarch64-linux-gnu-gcc"
   166вҶ’
   167вҶ’[tasks."build:arm64"]
   168вҶ’description = "Cross-compile for ARM64 Linux"
   169вҶ’run = "cargo build --release --target aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu"
   170вҶ’```
   171вҶ’
   172вҶ’For WASM targets:
   173вҶ’
   174вҶ’```toml
   175вҶ’[tasks."build:wasm"]
   176вҶ’description = "Build for WASM"
   177вҶ’run = "cargo build --release --target wasm32-unknown-unknown"
   178вҶ’```
   179вҶ’
   180вҶ’## Cargo Tool Extensions
   181вҶ’
   182вҶ’Install additional Cargo tools through mise backends:
   183вҶ’
   184вҶ’```toml
   185вҶ’[tools]
   186вҶ’"cargo:cargo-watch" = "latest"
   187вҶ’"cargo:cargo-nextest" = "latest"
   188вҶ’"cargo:cargo-audit" = "latest"
   189вҶ’```
   190вҶ’
   191вҶ’These install as mise-managed tools, separate from the project's `Cargo.toml` dependencies.
   192вҶ’

1вҶ’# mise: Examples
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’Complete `mise.toml` files for common project types. Copy and adapt as needed.
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’## Common Base Tasks
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’Standard boilerplate tasks that appear across most projects. Include the relevant subset when onboarding a repo.
     8вҶ’
     9вҶ’```toml
    10вҶ’min_version = "2024.9.5"
    11вҶ’
    12вҶ’[settings]
    13вҶ’auto_install = true
    14вҶ’not_found_auto_install = true
    15вҶ’task_run_auto_install = true
    16вҶ’
    17вҶ’[settings.status]
    18вҶ’show_tools = true
    19вҶ’show_env = false
    20вҶ’truncate = true
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’[tools]
    23вҶ’git-cliff = "2.10.1"
    24вҶ’prek = "0.2.20"
    25вҶ’"pipx:rumdl" = "latest"
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’# === Changelog ===
    28вҶ’[tasks.changelog]
    29вҶ’description = "Update changelog using git-cliff"
    30вҶ’run = "git-cliff -o CHANGELOG.md"
    31вҶ’
    32вҶ’# === Hooks ===
    33вҶ’[tasks.hooks-install]
    34вҶ’description = "Install pre-commit and infisical hooks"
    35вҶ’run = "prek install -f && infisical scan install --pre-commit-hook"
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’[tasks.pre-commit-run]
    38вҶ’description = "Run pre-commit hooks"
    39вҶ’run = "prek run --all-files"
    40вҶ’
    41вҶ’# === Security ===
    42вҶ’[tasks.infisical-scan]
    43вҶ’description = "Scan repository for secrets"
    44вҶ’run = "infisical scan"
    45вҶ’
    46вҶ’# === Markdown ===
    47вҶ’[tasks.markdown-lint]
    48вҶ’description = "Lint Markdown files"
    49вҶ’run = "rumdl check ."
    50вҶ’
    51вҶ’[tasks.markdown-fix]
    52вҶ’description = "Fix Markdown files"
    53вҶ’run = "rumdl fmt ."
    54вҶ’
    55вҶ’# === Composite ===
    56вҶ’[tasks.setup]
    57вҶ’description = "Set up development environment"
    58вҶ’depends = ["hooks-install"]
    59вҶ’```
    60вҶ’
    61вҶ’## Minimal Starter
    62вҶ’
    63вҶ’Bare-minimum configuration for any project. Add tools and tasks as the project grows.
    64вҶ’
    65вҶ’```toml
    66вҶ’[tools]
    67вҶ’node = "22"
    68вҶ’
    69вҶ’[tasks]
    70вҶ’dev = "npm run dev"
    71вҶ’test = "npm test"
    72вҶ’```
    73вҶ’
    74вҶ’## Python Web Application
    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’Full configuration for a Python web project with FastAPI, database migrations, and standard quality tooling.
    77вҶ’
    78вҶ’```toml
    79вҶ’[env]
    80вҶ’DATABASE_URL = "postgres://localhost/myapp_dev"
    81вҶ’_.python.venv = { path = ".venv", create = true }
    82вҶ’
    83вҶ’[tools]
    84вҶ’python = "3.12"
    85вҶ’
    86вҶ’[tasks]
    87вҶ’serve.run = "uvicorn app.main:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000"
    88вҶ’serve.description = "Run development server"
    89вҶ’
    90вҶ’test.run = "pytest"
    91вҶ’test.description = "Run test suite"
    92вҶ’
    93вҶ’"test:coverage".run = "pytest --cov=src --cov-report=term-missing"
    94вҶ’"test:coverage".description = "Run tests with coverage"
    95вҶ’
    96вҶ’lint.run = "ruff check ."
    97вҶ’lint.description = "Check code style"
    98вҶ’
    99вҶ’"lint:fix".run = "ruff check --fix ."
   100вҶ’format.run = "ruff format ."
   101вҶ’typecheck.run = "mypy src/"
   102вҶ’
   103вҶ’[tasks.check]
   104вҶ’description = "Full quality gate"
   105вҶ’depends = ["lint", "typecheck", "test"]
   106вҶ’
   107вҶ’[tasks."db:migrate"]
   108вҶ’description = "Run database migrations"
   109вҶ’run = "alembic upgrade head"
   110вҶ’
   111вҶ’[tasks."db:revision"]
   112вҶ’description = "Create new migration"
   113вҶ’usage = 'arg "<message>" help="Migration description"'
   114вҶ’run = "alembic revision --autogenerate -m \"$1\""
   115вҶ’
   116вҶ’[settings]
   117вҶ’experimental = true
   118вҶ’```
   119вҶ’
   120вҶ’## Rust CLI Tool
   121вҶ’
   122вҶ’Configuration for a Rust CLI project with standard Cargo workflows and source tracking.
   123вҶ’
   124вҶ’```toml
   125вҶ’[env]
   126вҶ’RUST_LOG = "info"
   127вҶ’
   128вҶ’[tools]
   129вҶ’rust = { version = "stable", components = "clippy,rustfmt" }
   130вҶ’"cargo:cargo-watch" = "latest"
   131вҶ’
   132вҶ’[tasks]
   133вҶ’build.run = "cargo build"
   134вҶ’build.description = "Build in debug mode"
   135вҶ’
   136вҶ’"build:release".run = "cargo build --release"
   137вҶ’"build:release".description = "Build optimized release binary"
   138вҶ’"build:release".sources = ["src/**/*.rs", "Cargo.toml", "Cargo.lock"]
   139вҶ’
   140вҶ’test.run = "cargo test"
   141вҶ’test.description = "Run test suite"
   142вҶ’
   143вҶ’lint.run = "cargo clippy -- -D warnings"
   144вҶ’lint.description = "Run clippy with strict warnings"
   145вҶ’
   146вҶ’format.run = "cargo fmt"
   147вҶ’"format:check".run = "cargo fmt -- --check"
   148вҶ’
   149вҶ’watch.run = "cargo watch -x build"
   150вҶ’watch.description = "Rebuild on file changes"
   151вҶ’
   152вҶ’run.run = "cargo run"
   153вҶ’run.depends = ["build"]
   154вҶ’
   155вҶ’install.run = "cargo install --path ."
   156вҶ’
   157вҶ’[tasks.check]
   158вҶ’description = "Full quality gate"
   159вҶ’depends = ["format:check", "lint", "test"]
   160вҶ’
   161вҶ’[settings]
   162вҶ’experimental = true
   163вҶ’```
   164вҶ’
   165вҶ’## Multi-Language Project
   166вҶ’
   167вҶ’Python backend with Node.js tooling (documentation site, pre-commit hooks, or frontend build).
   168вҶ’
   169вҶ’```toml
   170вҶ’[env]
   171вҶ’_.python.venv = { path = ".venv", create = true }
   172вҶ’
   173вҶ’[tools]
   174вҶ’python = "3.12"
   175вҶ’node = "22"
   176вҶ’
   177вҶ’[tasks]
   178вҶ’test.run = "pytest"
   179вҶ’lint.run = "ruff check . && eslint docs/"
   180вҶ’format.run = ["ruff format .", "prettier --write docs/"]
   181вҶ’
   182вҶ’"docs:dev".run = "npm run docs:dev"
   183вҶ’"docs:dev".description = "Run docs development server"
   184вҶ’
   185вҶ’"docs:build".run = "npm run docs:build"
   186вҶ’"docs:build".description = "Build documentation site"
   187вҶ’
   188вҶ’[tasks.check]
   189вҶ’description = "Full quality gate"
   190вҶ’depends = ["lint", "test", "docs:build"]
   191вҶ’
   192вҶ’[settings]
   193вҶ’experimental = true
   194вҶ’```
   195вҶ’
   196вҶ’## Infrastructure as Code (Terraform)
   197вҶ’
   198вҶ’Configuration for a Terraform project with environment-scoped validation, linting, and operations. Demonstrates `dir` for targeting subdirectories.
   199вҶ’
   200вҶ’```toml
   201вҶ’[settings]
   202вҶ’auto_install = true
   203вҶ’not_found_auto_install = true
   204вҶ’jobs = 4
   205вҶ’
   206вҶ’[settings.status]
   207вҶ’show_tools = true
   208вҶ’
   209вҶ’[tools]
   210вҶ’terraform = "1.14"
   211вҶ’terraform-docs = "0.21"
   212вҶ’tflint = "0.61"
   213вҶ’yamllint = "1.38"
   214вҶ’yamlfmt = "0.21"
   215вҶ’shellcheck = "0.11"
   216вҶ’
   217вҶ’[tasks.fmt]
   218вҶ’description = "Format all Terraform files recursively"
   219вҶ’dir = "infrastructure"
   220вҶ’run = "terraform fmt -recursive"
   221вҶ’
   222вҶ’[tasks.fmt-check]
   223вҶ’description = "Check formatting (CI-friendly)"
   224вҶ’dir = "infrastructure"
   225вҶ’run = "terraform fmt -recursive -check -diff"
   226вҶ’
   227вҶ’[tasks.yaml-fmt]
   228вҶ’description = "Format YAML files"
   229вҶ’run = "yamlfmt ."
   230вҶ’
   231вҶ’[tasks.prod-validate]
   232вҶ’description = "Validate production Terraform"
   233вҶ’dir = "infrastructure/environments/production"
   234вҶ’run = "terraform init -backend=false -input=false >/dev/null && terraform validate"
   235вҶ’
   236вҶ’[tasks.prod-plan]
   237вҶ’description = "Plan production changes"
   238вҶ’dir = "infrastructure/environments/production"
   239вҶ’run = "terraform plan"
   240вҶ’
   241вҶ’[tasks.prod-apply]
   242вҶ’description = "Apply production changes"
   243вҶ’dir = "infrastructure/environments/production"
   244вҶ’run = "terraform apply"
   245вҶ’
   246вҶ’[tasks.lint-prod]
   247вҶ’description = "Run TFLint in production"
   248вҶ’dir = "infrastructure/environments/production"
   249вҶ’run = "tflint --init && tflint"
   250вҶ’
   251вҶ’[tasks.shellcheck]
   252вҶ’description = "Lint shell scripts"
   253вҶ’run = "find scripts -name '*.sh' -exec shellcheck {} +"
   254вҶ’
   255вҶ’[tasks.docs]
   256вҶ’description = "Generate Terraform documentation"
   257вҶ’run = "./scripts/generate-docs.sh"
   258вҶ’
   259вҶ’[tasks.check]
   260вҶ’description = "Format, lint, and validate"
   261вҶ’depends = ["fmt", "lint-prod", "prod-validate"]
   262вҶ’env = { TF_IN_AUTOMATION = "true", TF_INPUT = "false" }
   263вҶ’
   264вҶ’[tasks.full-check]
   265вҶ’description = "Complete validation suite"
   266вҶ’depends = ["fmt", "yaml-fmt", "prod-validate", "lint-prod", "shellcheck", "docs"]
   267вҶ’env = { TF_IN_AUTOMATION = "true", TF_INPUT = "false" }
   268вҶ’
   269вҶ’[tasks.setup]
   270вҶ’description = "Initial project setup"
   271вҶ’depends = ["docs"]
   272вҶ’```
   273вҶ’
   274вҶ’## Claude Code Integration
   275вҶ’
   276вҶ’Tasks that wrap Claude Code CLI for AI-assisted workflows. Add to a project's `mise.toml` or a global `~/.config/mise/config.toml`.
   277вҶ’
   278вҶ’```toml
   279вҶ’[tasks."cc:ask"]
   280вҶ’description = "One-shot question to Claude about this project"
   281вҶ’run = "claude --print \"$1\""
   282вҶ’
   283вҶ’[tasks."cc:review"]
   284вҶ’description = "AI review of staged changes"
   285вҶ’run = "git diff --staged | claude --print 'Review these changes for bugs, style issues, and security concerns.'"
   286вҶ’
   287вҶ’[tasks."cc:explain"]
   288вҶ’description = "Explain a file using Claude"
   289вҶ’usage = 'arg "<file>" help="File to explain"'
   290вҶ’run = "cat \"$1\" | claude --print 'Explain this file. Focus on architecture and key decisions.'"
   291вҶ’
   292вҶ’[settings]
   293вҶ’experimental = true
   294вҶ’```
   295вҶ’
   296вҶ’For global cross-repo tasks, place in `~/.config/mise/config.toml`:
   297вҶ’
   298вҶ’```toml
   299вҶ’[tasks."repo:status"]
   300вҶ’description = "Show git state of all managed repos"
   301вҶ’run = '''
   302вҶ’for repo in ~/dev/projects/*/; do
   303вҶ’  if [ -d "$repo/.git" ]; then
   304вҶ’    printf "%-30s %s\n" "$(basename $repo)" "$(git -C $repo status --short | head -1)"
   305вҶ’  fi
   306вҶ’done
   307вҶ’'''
   308вҶ’```
   309вҶ’

1вҶ’# repo-forge
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’Project setup and validation toolkit. Ensures repos are properly configured
     4вҶ’with standard tooling across Python, Rust, Terraform, and Ansible projects.
     5вҶ’
     6вҶ’## Skills
     7вҶ’
     8вҶ’| Skill | Status | Purpose |
     9вҶ’|-------|--------|---------|
    10вҶ’| `mise` | Available | Config patterns, task definitions, language-specific setup |
    11вҶ’| `pre-commit` | Planned | Hook configuration and repo setup |
    12вҶ’| `gitignore` | Planned | Template generation and validation per project type |
    13вҶ’| `git-cliff` | Planned | Changelog config and conventional commit conventions |
    14вҶ’| `project-setup` | Planned | Project type identification and orchestrated setup |
    15вҶ’

1вҶ’---
     2вҶ’name: mise
     3вҶ’description: >-
     4вҶ’  This skill provides mise configuration patterns, task definitions, and language-specific
     5вҶ’  setup guidance. This skill should be used when the user asks to "set up mise", "create a
     6вҶ’  mise.toml", "configure task runner", "manage tool versions", "add a task to mise",
     7вҶ’  "set up Python with mise", "configure Rust toolchain", "onboard a repo", "add mise to this
     8вҶ’  project", "set up dev environment", or mentions mise configuration, dev tooling setup,
     9вҶ’  task definitions, or project onboarding.
    10вҶ’---
    11вҶ’
    12вҶ’# mise
    13вҶ’
    14вҶ’mise is a polyglot tool version manager and task runner. A single `mise.toml` replaces Makefiles, shell scripts, and language-specific version managers (nvm, pyenv, rustup). It manages tool versions, defines project tasks, and sets environment variables from one configuration file.
    15вҶ’
    16вҶ’## Config File Structure
    17вҶ’
    18вҶ’Order sections canonically for consistency:
    19вҶ’
    20вҶ’```toml
    21вҶ’min_version = "2024.9.5"
    22вҶ’
    23вҶ’[env]
    24вҶ’DATABASE_URL = "postgres://localhost/myapp_dev"
    25вҶ’
    26вҶ’[tools]
    27вҶ’python = "3.12"
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’[tasks]
    30вҶ’test.run = "pytest"
    31вҶ’
    32вҶ’[settings]
    33вҶ’auto_install = true
    34вҶ’not_found_auto_install = true
    35вҶ’task_run_auto_install = true
    36вҶ’```
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’Use `min_version` to enforce a minimum mise version. Recognized top-level sections: `[env]`, `[tools]`, `[tasks]`, `[settings]`, `[plugins]`, `[tool_alias]`, `[task_config]`, `[vars]`.
    39вҶ’
    40вҶ’## Tool Version Management
    41вҶ’
    42вҶ’Specify tools in the `[tools]` section. mise installs and activates them automatically.
    43вҶ’
    44вҶ’```toml
    45вҶ’[tools]
    46вҶ’node = "22"                   # Major version prefix
    47вҶ’python = "3.12.2"             # Exact version
    48вҶ’go = "latest"                 # Latest stable
    49вҶ’erlang = "lts"                # LTS channel
    50вҶ’```
    51вҶ’
    52вҶ’Multiple versions install side-by-side (first is default):
    53вҶ’
    54вҶ’```toml
    55вҶ’[tools]
    56вҶ’python = ["3.11", "3.12"]
    57вҶ’```
    58вҶ’
    59вҶ’### Version formats
    60вҶ’
    61вҶ’| Format | Example | Behavior |
    62вҶ’|--------|---------|----------|
    63вҶ’| Exact | `"3.12.2"` | Specific version |
    64вҶ’| Prefix | `"3.12"` | Latest matching 3.12.x |
    65вҶ’| Channel | `"latest"`, `"lts"` | Resolved at install time |
    66вҶ’| Reference | `"ref:main"` | Git ref, compiled from source |
    67вҶ’| Subtraction | `"sub-1:latest"` | One major behind latest |
    68вҶ’
    69вҶ’### Table form with options
    70вҶ’
    71вҶ’```toml
    72вҶ’[tools]
    73вҶ’node = { version = "22", postinstall = "corepack enable" }
    74вҶ’rust = { version = "stable", components = "clippy,rustfmt", profile = "default" }
    75вҶ’```
    76вҶ’
    77вҶ’### Backend syntax for non-core tools
    78вҶ’
    79вҶ’```toml
    80вҶ’[tools]
    81вҶ’"ubi:astral-sh/ruff" = "latest"     # GitHub binary releases
    82вҶ’"cargo:cargo-watch" = "latest"       # Cargo crate
    83вҶ’"npm:prettier" = "3"                 # npm package
    84вҶ’"pipx:ansible-lint" = "latest"       # Python CLI tools via pipx
    85вҶ’```
    86вҶ’
    87вҶ’### Custom plugins
    88вҶ’
    89вҶ’Register custom tool plugins in `[plugins]`:
    90вҶ’
    91вҶ’```toml
    92вҶ’[plugins]
    93вҶ’fnox-env = "https://github.com/jdx/mise-env-fnox"
    94вҶ’```
    95вҶ’
    96вҶ’## Task Definitions
    97вҶ’
    98вҶ’Tasks are defined in `[tasks]` or as executable files in `.mise/tasks/`.
    99вҶ’
   100вҶ’### Inline tasks
   101вҶ’
   102вҶ’```toml
   103вҶ’tasks.test = "pytest"
   104вҶ’tasks.lint = "ruff check ."
   105вҶ’tasks.format = "ruff format ."
   106вҶ’```
   107вҶ’
   108вҶ’### Table tasks
   109вҶ’
   110вҶ’```toml
   111вҶ’[tasks.build]
   112вҶ’description = "Build the project"
   113вҶ’depends = ["lint"]
   114вҶ’run = "cargo build --release"
   115вҶ’dir = "{{config_root}}/backend"
   116вҶ’env = { RUST_LOG = "info" }
   117вҶ’sources = ["src/**/*.rs", "Cargo.toml"]
   118вҶ’outputs = ["target/release/myapp"]
   119вҶ’```
   120вҶ’
   121вҶ’### Task-level options
   122вҶ’
   123вҶ’Pin tool versions per-task, set aliases for shortcuts, or override the shell:
   124вҶ’
   125вҶ’```toml
   126вҶ’[tasks."lint:shellcheck"]
   127вҶ’description = "Lint shell scripts"
   128вҶ’run = "shellcheck scripts/*.sh"
   129вҶ’tools = { "shellcheck" = "0.11.0" }    # Pin tool version for this task
   130вҶ’alias = "sc"                            # Short alias for mise run sc
   131вҶ’shell = "bash -c"                       # Override default shell
   132вҶ’```
   133вҶ’
   134вҶ’### Task directory scoping
   135вҶ’
   136вҶ’Use `dir` to run a task in a specific subdirectory. Essential for monorepos and infrastructure projects where different tasks target different paths:
   137вҶ’
   138вҶ’```toml
   139вҶ’[tasks.prod-validate]
   140вҶ’description = "Validate production Terraform"
   141вҶ’dir = "infrastructure/environments/production"
   142вҶ’run = "terraform init -backend=false -input=false >/dev/null && terraform validate"
   143вҶ’
   144вҶ’[tasks.prod-plan]
   145вҶ’description = "Plan production changes"
   146вҶ’dir = "infrastructure/environments/production"
   147вҶ’run = "terraform plan"
   148вҶ’```
   149вҶ’
   150вҶ’Relative paths resolve from the config file's directory. Use `{{config_root}}` for explicit anchoring. When multiple tasks share a base directory, the pattern of environment-scoped tasks (same command, different `dir`) keeps configs DRY while remaining explicit about scope.
   151вҶ’
   152вҶ’### Multi-line scripts
   153вҶ’
   154вҶ’```toml
   155вҶ’[tasks.setup]
   156вҶ’run = [
   157вҶ’  "pip install -e '.[dev]'",
   158вҶ’  "pre-commit install"
   159вҶ’]
   160вҶ’```
   161вҶ’
   162вҶ’### File-based tasks
   163вҶ’
   164вҶ’Place executable scripts in `.mise/tasks/`. Subdirectories create namespaces вҖ” `.mise/tasks/db/migrate` becomes task `db:migrate`.
   165вҶ’
   166вҶ’```bash
   167вҶ’#!/usr/bin/env bash
   168вҶ’#MISE description="Run database migrations"
   169вҶ’#MISE depends=["db:check"]
   170вҶ’
   171вҶ’alembic upgrade head
   172вҶ’```
   173вҶ’
   174вҶ’Mark file tasks executable (`chmod +x`). Any language works via shebang.
   175вҶ’
   176вҶ’### Task arguments
   177вҶ’
   178вҶ’```toml
   179вҶ’[tasks.deploy]
   180вҶ’usage = '''
   181вҶ’arg "<environment>" help="Target environment" default="staging"
   182вҶ’flag "-v --verbose" help="Verbose output"
   183вҶ’'''
   184вҶ’run = "deploy.sh ${usage_environment}"
   185вҶ’```
   186вҶ’
   187вҶ’## Namespace Taxonomy
   188вҶ’
   189вҶ’Name tasks as `namespace:verb`. The namespace is a domain noun, the verb is an action. This groups related tasks alphabetically in `mise tasks` output.
   190вҶ’
   191вҶ’```toml
   192вҶ’# Per-project namespaces use the project's domain
   193вҶ’tasks."vault:triage" = "claude --print /inbox-process"
   194вҶ’tasks."cluster:deploy" = "talosctl apply-config"
   195вҶ’
   196вҶ’# Cross-project namespaces for shared concerns
   197вҶ’tasks."cc:prime" = "claude --print /vault-prime"
   198вҶ’tasks."git:clean" = "git branch --merged | grep -v main | xargs git branch -d"
   199вҶ’```
   200вҶ’
   201вҶ’Use the project's primary domain noun as the namespace. Avoid generic names like `run:` or `do:`.
   202вҶ’
   203вҶ’## Environment Variables
   204вҶ’
   205вҶ’```toml
   206вҶ’[env]
   207вҶ’NODE_ENV = "development"
   208вҶ’PROJECT_ROOT = "{{config_root}}"
   209вҶ’
   210вҶ’# Load from dotenv
   211вҶ’_.file = ".env"
   212вҶ’
   213вҶ’# Python virtual environment
   214вҶ’_.python.venv = { path = ".venv", create = true }
   215вҶ’```
   216вҶ’
   217вҶ’Template variables: `{{config_root}}` (directory containing mise.toml), `{{env.VAR}}` (existing env vars).
   218вҶ’
   219вҶ’## Layered Configuration
   220вҶ’
   221вҶ’mise resolves configuration from multiple levels, with closer files taking precedence:
   222вҶ’
   223вҶ’```text
   224вҶ’~/.config/mise/config.toml       # Global: cross-cutting tools and tasks
   225вҶ’./mise.toml                      # Project: local tools and tasks
   226вҶ’./mise.local.toml                # Local overrides (gitignored)
   227вҶ’```
   228вҶ’
   229вҶ’Global config handles cross-cutting concerns (shared tools, repo orchestration tasks). Project config handles repo-specific tools and tasks. Global tasks never assume a specific repo structure вҖ” use environment variables or auto-detection.
   230вҶ’
   231вҶ’Use `mise.local.toml` (gitignored) for environment-specific overrides. Tasks can generate this file to switch environments:
   232вҶ’
   233вҶ’```toml
   234вҶ’[tasks."env:local"]
   235вҶ’description = "Switch to local LAN environment"
   236вҶ’run = '''
   237вҶ’cat > .mise.local.toml << 'EOF'
   238вҶ’[env]
   239вҶ’NOMAD_ADDR = "http://192.168.11.11:4646"
   240вҶ’CONSUL_HTTP_ADDR = "http://192.168.11.11:8500"
   241вҶ’EOF
   242вҶ’echo "Switched to local. Run 'mise trust' to apply."
   243вҶ’'''
   244вҶ’```
   245вҶ’
   246вҶ’## Task Dependencies
   247вҶ’
   248вҶ’```toml
   249вҶ’[tasks.check]
   250вҶ’description = "Full quality gate"
   251вҶ’depends = ["format:check", "lint", "test"]
   252вҶ’
   253вҶ’[tasks.deploy]
   254вҶ’depends = ["check"]
   255вҶ’depends_post = ["notify"]           # Runs after this task completes
   256вҶ’wait_for = ["build"]                # Waits without forcing execution
   257вҶ’```
   258вҶ’
   259вҶ’Independent dependencies run in parallel by default. Control parallelism with `[settings] jobs = 4`.
   260вҶ’
   261вҶ’## Settings
   262вҶ’
   263вҶ’```toml
   264вҶ’[settings]
   265вҶ’experimental = true                 # Required for file-based tasks
   266вҶ’jobs = 4                            # Parallel task execution
   267вҶ’task_output = "prefix"              # prefix | interleave | quiet | silent
   268вҶ’```
   269вҶ’
   270вҶ’Language-specific settings nest under the tool name: `python.compile`, `rust.cargo_home`. See language reference files for details.
   271вҶ’
   272вҶ’## Claude Code Integration
   273вҶ’
   274вҶ’mise tasks orchestrate shell-side operations around Claude Code sessions. The pattern: mise owns the shell, Claude owns the conversation. When a mise task needs Claude, invoke the CLI with `claude --print`. Reserve interactive `claude` for workflows requiring user interaction. Never reimplement slash command logic in mise вҖ” wrap it.
   275вҶ’
   276вҶ’See [Examples](examples/examples.md) for complete Claude Code integration task patterns.
   277вҶ’
   278вҶ’## Reference Guides
   279вҶ’
   280вҶ’- [Python Ecosystem](references/python.md) вҖ” Version management, venv handling, and task patterns for Python projects
   281вҶ’- [Rust Ecosystem](references/rust.md) вҖ” Toolchain management and task patterns for Rust projects
   282вҶ’- [Examples](examples/examples.md) вҖ” Complete `mise.toml` files for common project types
   283вҶ’
   284вҶ’## External Resources
   285вҶ’
   286вҶ’- [mise documentation](https://mise.jdx.dev/) вҖ” Official docs
   287вҶ’- [mise GitHub](https://github.com/jdx/mise) вҖ” Source repository
   288вҶ’- [mise.toml schema](https://mise.jdx.dev/schema/mise.json) вҖ” JSON schema for editor validation
   289вҶ’

1вҶ’# mise: Rust Ecosystem
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’Rust toolchain management and common task patterns for Rust projects.
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’## Rust Toolchain Management
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’mise manages Rust through rustup. Installations live in rustup's directory structure, not mise's. mise sets `RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN` to activate the correct version.
     8вҶ’
     9вҶ’```toml
    10вҶ’[tools]
    11вҶ’rust = "stable"               # Most common
    12вҶ’```
    13вҶ’
    14вҶ’Other channel options:
    15вҶ’
    16вҶ’```toml
    17вҶ’[tools]
    18вҶ’rust = "1.83"                 # Pinned version
    19вҶ’# or
    20вҶ’rust = "nightly"              # Nightly channel
    21вҶ’```
    22вҶ’
    23вҶ’### Components and profiles
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’```toml
    26вҶ’[tools]
    27вҶ’rust = {
    28вҶ’  version = "stable",
    29вҶ’  components = "clippy,rustfmt,rust-src",
    30вҶ’  profile = "default"
    31вҶ’}
    32вҶ’```
    33вҶ’
    34вҶ’Profile options: `minimal` (compiler only), `default` (plus docs, rustfmt, clippy), `complete` (all components).
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’### Cross-compilation targets
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’```toml
    39вҶ’[tools]
    40вҶ’rust = {
    41вҶ’  version = "stable",
    42вҶ’  targets = "wasm32-unknown-unknown,aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu"
    43вҶ’}
    44вҶ’```
    45вҶ’
    46вҶ’### When to use mise vs rustup directly
    47вҶ’
    48вҶ’Use mise for version pinning in `mise.toml` вҖ” the project declares which Rust version it needs, and mise activates it. Rustup handles the underlying installation, component management, and target configuration. The two work together: mise selects, rustup provides.
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’### Rust settings
    51вҶ’
    52вҶ’```toml
    53вҶ’[settings]
    54вҶ’rust.cargo_home = "~/.cargo"
    55вҶ’rust.rustup_home = "~/.rustup"
    56вҶ’```
    57вҶ’
    58вҶ’These isolate mise's Rust installation from other rustup setups on the system when needed.
    59вҶ’
    60вҶ’## Task Patterns
    61вҶ’
    62вҶ’### Building
    63вҶ’
    64вҶ’```toml
    65вҶ’[tasks.build]
    66вҶ’description = "Build in debug mode"
    67вҶ’run = "cargo build"
    68вҶ’
    69вҶ’[tasks."build:release"]
    70вҶ’description = "Build optimized release binary"
    71вҶ’run = "cargo build --release"
    72вҶ’sources = ["src/**/*.rs", "Cargo.toml", "Cargo.lock"]
    73вҶ’outputs = ["target/release/{{env.CARGO_BIN_NAME}}"]
    74вҶ’```
    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’### Testing
    77вҶ’
    78вҶ’```toml
    79вҶ’[tasks.test]
    80вҶ’description = "Run test suite"
    81вҶ’run = "cargo test"
    82вҶ’
    83вҶ’[tasks."test:verbose"]
    84вҶ’description = "Run tests with output"
    85вҶ’run = "cargo test -- --nocapture"
    86вҶ’
    87вҶ’[tasks."test:doc"]
    88вҶ’description = "Run documentation tests"
    89вҶ’run = "cargo test --doc"
    90вҶ’```
    91вҶ’
    92вҶ’### Linting and Formatting
    93вҶ’
    94вҶ’```toml
    95вҶ’[tasks.lint]
    96вҶ’description = "Run clippy with strict warnings"
    97вҶ’run = "cargo clippy -- -D warnings"
    98вҶ’
    99вҶ’[tasks.format]
   100вҶ’description = "Format code"
   101вҶ’run = "cargo fmt"
   102вҶ’
   103вҶ’[tasks."format:check"]
   104вҶ’description = "Check formatting without changes"
   105вҶ’run = "cargo fmt -- --check"
   106вҶ’```
   107вҶ’
   108вҶ’### Quality Gate
   109вҶ’
   110вҶ’```toml
   111вҶ’[tasks.check]
   112вҶ’description = "Full quality gate"
   113вҶ’depends = ["format:check", "lint", "test"]
   114вҶ’```
   115вҶ’
   116вҶ’### Documentation
   117вҶ’
   118вҶ’```toml
   119вҶ’[tasks.doc]
   120вҶ’description = "Build and open docs"
   121вҶ’run = "cargo doc --open --no-deps"
   122вҶ’
   123вҶ’[tasks."doc:check"]
   124вҶ’description = "Check doc warnings"
   125вҶ’run = "RUSTDOCFLAGS='-D warnings' cargo doc --no-deps"
   126вҶ’```
   127вҶ’
   128вҶ’### Development Workflow
   129вҶ’
   130вҶ’```toml
   131вҶ’[tasks.watch]
   132вҶ’description = "Rebuild on file changes"
   133вҶ’run = "cargo watch -x build"
   134вҶ’
   135вҶ’[tasks.run]
   136вҶ’description = "Run the binary"
   137вҶ’depends = ["build"]
   138вҶ’run = "cargo run"
   139вҶ’
   140вҶ’[tasks.install]
   141вҶ’description = "Install binary locally"
   142вҶ’run = "cargo install --path ."
   143вҶ’```
   144вҶ’
   145вҶ’### Workspace Tasks
   146вҶ’
   147вҶ’For Cargo workspaces with multiple crates:
   148вҶ’
   149вҶ’```toml
   150вҶ’[tasks."ws:test"]
   151вҶ’description = "Test all workspace members"
   152вҶ’run = "cargo test --workspace"
   153вҶ’
   154вҶ’[tasks."ws:check"]
   155вҶ’description = "Check all workspace members"
   156вҶ’run = "cargo check --workspace"
   157вҶ’```
   158вҶ’
   159вҶ’## Cross-Compilation
   160вҶ’
   161вҶ’Set target-specific environment variables through `[env]`:
   162вҶ’
   163вҶ’```toml
   164вҶ’[env]
   165вҶ’CARGO_TARGET_AARCH64_UNKNOWN_LINUX_GNU_LINKER = "aarch64-linux-gnu-gcc"
   166вҶ’
   167вҶ’[tasks."build:arm64"]
   168вҶ’description = "Cross-compile for ARM64 Linux"
   169вҶ’run = "cargo build --release --target aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu"
   170вҶ’```
   171вҶ’
   172вҶ’For WASM targets:
   173вҶ’
   174вҶ’```toml
   175вҶ’[tasks."build:wasm"]
   176вҶ’description = "Build for WASM"
   177вҶ’run = "cargo build --release --target wasm32-unknown-unknown"
   178вҶ’```
   179вҶ’
   180вҶ’## Cargo Tool Extensions
   181вҶ’
   182вҶ’Install additional Cargo tools through mise backends:
   183вҶ’
   184вҶ’```toml
   185вҶ’[tools]
   186вҶ’"cargo:cargo-watch" = "latest"
   187вҶ’"cargo:cargo-nextest" = "latest"
   188вҶ’"cargo:cargo-audit" = "latest"
   189вҶ’```
   190вҶ’
   191вҶ’These install as mise-managed tools, separate from the project's `Cargo.toml` dependencies.
   192вҶ’

1вҶ’# mise: Python Ecosystem
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’Python version management, virtual environment handling, and common task patterns for Python projects.
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’## Python Version Management
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’mise manages Python versions through its core plugin. No separate plugin install required.
     8вҶ’
     9вҶ’```toml
    10вҶ’[tools]
    11вҶ’python = "3.12"               # Latest 3.12.x (prefix match)
    12вҶ’```
    13вҶ’
    14вҶ’Other version formats:
    15вҶ’
    16вҶ’```toml
    17вҶ’[tools]
    18вҶ’python = "3.12.4"             # Exact version
    19вҶ’# or
    20вҶ’python = ["3.11", "3.12"]     # Multiple versions (first is default)
    21вҶ’```
    22вҶ’
    23вҶ’By default, mise installs precompiled Python binaries for speed. To compile from source (needed for custom build flags or uncommon platforms):
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’```toml
    26вҶ’[settings]
    27вҶ’python.compile = true
    28вҶ’```
    29вҶ’
    30вҶ’### Idiomatic version files
    31вҶ’
    32вҶ’mise reads `.python-version` files when idiomatic version file support is enabled, providing compatibility with pyenv-based workflows.
    33вҶ’
    34вҶ’## Virtual Environments
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’Configure automatic venv creation and activation through the `[env]` section:
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’```toml
    39вҶ’[env]
    40вҶ’_.python.venv = { path = ".venv", create = true }
    41вҶ’```
    42вҶ’
    43вҶ’This creates `.venv` if it doesn't exist and activates it when entering the project directory.
    44вҶ’
    45вҶ’### Extended options
    46вҶ’
    47вҶ’```toml
    48вҶ’[env]
    49вҶ’_.python.venv = {
    50вҶ’  path = ".venv",
    51вҶ’  create = true,
    52вҶ’  python = "3.11",                          # Pin venv to specific version
    53вҶ’  uv_create_args = ["--seed"]               # Pass args to uv venv
    54вҶ’}
    55вҶ’```
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’### UV integration
    58вҶ’
    59вҶ’To align UV's Python resolution with the mise-managed version:
    60вҶ’
    61вҶ’```toml
    62вҶ’[env]
    63вҶ’UV_PYTHON = { value = "{{ tools.python.path }}", tools = true }
    64вҶ’```
    65вҶ’
    66вҶ’### Settings
    67вҶ’
    68вҶ’```toml
    69вҶ’[settings]
    70вҶ’python.uv_venv_auto = false                # false | "source" | "create|source" | true
    71вҶ’python.venv_stdlib = false                  # Use stdlib venv instead of uv/virtualenv
    72вҶ’```
    73вҶ’
    74вҶ’## uv Integration
    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’Most Python projects use uv as the package manager. Prefix commands with `uv run` to execute through the managed venv:
    77вҶ’
    78вҶ’```toml
    79вҶ’[tasks.python-install]
    80вҶ’description = "Install Python dependencies"
    81вҶ’run = "uv sync"
    82вҶ’
    83вҶ’[tasks.python-upgrade]
    84вҶ’description = "Upgrade Python dependencies"
    85вҶ’run = "uv lock --upgrade"
    86вҶ’
    87вҶ’[tasks.python-clean]
    88вҶ’description = "Clean development environment"
    89вҶ’run = ["rm -rf .venv", "rm -rf requirements.lock"]
    90вҶ’```
    91вҶ’
    92вҶ’For CI, use `uv sync --frozen` to install from the lockfile without updating it.
    93вҶ’
    94вҶ’## Task Patterns
    95вҶ’
    96вҶ’All task commands below assume uv-managed projects. Prefix with `uv run` when executing tools installed in the project venv.
    97вҶ’
    98вҶ’### Testing
    99вҶ’
   100вҶ’```toml
   101вҶ’[tasks.test]
   102вҶ’description = "Run test suite"
   103вҶ’run = "uv run pytest"
   104вҶ’
   105вҶ’[tasks."test:coverage"]
   106вҶ’description = "Run tests with coverage"
   107вҶ’run = "uv run pytest --cov=src --cov-report=term-missing"
   108вҶ’
   109вҶ’[tasks."test:watch"]
   110вҶ’description = "Run tests on file change"
   111вҶ’run = "uv run ptw -- -x"
   112вҶ’```
   113вҶ’
   114вҶ’### Linting and Formatting
   115вҶ’
   116вҶ’```toml
   117вҶ’[tasks.lint]
   118вҶ’description = "Check code style"
   119вҶ’run = "uv run ruff check ."
   120вҶ’
   121вҶ’[tasks."lint:fix"]
   122вҶ’description = "Auto-fix lint issues"
   123вҶ’run = "uv run ruff check --fix ."
   124вҶ’
   125вҶ’[tasks.format]
   126вҶ’description = "Format code"
   127вҶ’run = "uv run ruff format ."
   128вҶ’
   129вҶ’[tasks."format:check"]
   130вҶ’description = "Check formatting without changes"
   131вҶ’run = "uv run ruff format --check ."
   132вҶ’```
   133вҶ’
   134вҶ’### Type Checking
   135вҶ’
   136вҶ’```toml
   137вҶ’[tasks.typecheck]
   138вҶ’description = "Run type checker"
   139вҶ’run = "uv run mypy src/"
   140вҶ’```
   141вҶ’
   142вҶ’### Quality Gate
   143вҶ’
   144вҶ’```toml
   145вҶ’[tasks.check]
   146вҶ’description = "Full quality gate"
   147вҶ’depends = ["format:check", "lint", "typecheck", "test"]
   148вҶ’```
   149вҶ’
   150вҶ’### Packaging
   151вҶ’
   152вҶ’```toml
   153вҶ’[tasks.build]
   154вҶ’description = "Build distribution packages"
   155вҶ’depends = ["check"]
   156вҶ’run = "uv run python -m build"
   157вҶ’
   158вҶ’[tasks.publish]
   159вҶ’description = "Publish to PyPI"
   160вҶ’depends = ["build"]
   161вҶ’run = "uv run twine upload dist/*"
   162вҶ’```
   163вҶ’
   164вҶ’### Development Server
   165вҶ’
   166вҶ’```toml
   167вҶ’[tasks.serve]
   168вҶ’description = "Run development server"
   169вҶ’run = "uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000"
   170вҶ’
   171вҶ’[tasks."db:migrate"]
   172вҶ’description = "Run database migrations"
   173вҶ’run = "uv run alembic upgrade head"
   174вҶ’
   175вҶ’[tasks."db:revision"]
   176вҶ’description = "Create new migration"
   177вҶ’usage = 'arg "<message>" help="Migration description"'
   178вҶ’run = "uv run alembic revision --autogenerate -m \"$1\""
   179вҶ’```
   180вҶ’
   181вҶ’## Default Packages
   182вҶ’
   183вҶ’Create `~/.default-python-packages` with one package per line to auto-install into every new Python version:
   184вҶ’
   185вҶ’```text
   186вҶ’pip
   187вҶ’setuptools
   188вҶ’wheel
   189вҶ’pipx
   190вҶ’```
   191вҶ’
   192вҶ’Set `MISE_PYTHON_DEFAULT_PACKAGES_FILE` to use a custom path.
   193вҶ’

1вҶ’# mise: Examples
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’Complete `mise.toml` files for common project types. Copy and adapt as needed.
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’## Common Base Tasks
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’Standard boilerplate tasks that appear across most projects. Include the relevant subset when onboarding a repo.
     8вҶ’
     9вҶ’```toml
    10вҶ’min_version = "2024.9.5"
    11вҶ’
    12вҶ’[settings]
    13вҶ’auto_install = true
    14вҶ’not_found_auto_install = true
    15вҶ’task_run_auto_install = true
    16вҶ’
    17вҶ’[settings.status]
    18вҶ’show_tools = true
    19вҶ’show_env = false
    20вҶ’truncate = true
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’[tools]
    23вҶ’git-cliff = "2.10.1"
    24вҶ’prek = "0.2.20"
    25вҶ’"pipx:rumdl" = "latest"
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’# === Changelog ===
    28вҶ’[tasks.changelog]
    29вҶ’description = "Update changelog using git-cliff"
    30вҶ’run = "git-cliff -o CHANGELOG.md"
    31вҶ’
    32вҶ’# === Hooks ===
    33вҶ’[tasks.hooks-install]
    34вҶ’description = "Install pre-commit and infisical hooks"
    35вҶ’run = "prek install -f && infisical scan install --pre-commit-hook"
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’[tasks.pre-commit-run]
    38вҶ’description = "Run pre-commit hooks"
    39вҶ’run = "prek run --all-files"
    40вҶ’
    41вҶ’# === Security ===
    42вҶ’[tasks.infisical-scan]
    43вҶ’description = "Scan repository for secrets"
    44вҶ’run = "infisical scan"
    45вҶ’
    46вҶ’# === Markdown ===
    47вҶ’[tasks.markdown-lint]
    48вҶ’description = "Lint Markdown files"
    49вҶ’run = "rumdl check ."
    50вҶ’
    51вҶ’[tasks.markdown-fix]
    52вҶ’description = "Fix Markdown files"
    53вҶ’run = "rumdl fmt ."
    54вҶ’
    55вҶ’# === Composite ===
    56вҶ’[tasks.setup]
    57вҶ’description = "Set up development environment"
    58вҶ’depends = ["hooks-install"]
    59вҶ’```
    60вҶ’
    61вҶ’## Minimal Starter
    62вҶ’
    63вҶ’Bare-minimum configuration for any project. Add tools and tasks as the project grows.
    64вҶ’
    65вҶ’```toml
    66вҶ’[tools]
    67вҶ’node = "22"
    68вҶ’
    69вҶ’[tasks]
    70вҶ’dev = "npm run dev"
    71вҶ’test = "npm test"
    72вҶ’```
    73вҶ’
    74вҶ’## Python Web Application
    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’Full configuration for a Python web project with FastAPI, database migrations, and standard quality tooling.
    77вҶ’
    78вҶ’```toml
    79вҶ’[env]
    80вҶ’DATABASE_URL = "postgres://localhost/myapp_dev"
    81вҶ’_.python.venv = { path = ".venv", create = true }
    82вҶ’
    83вҶ’[tools]
    84вҶ’python = "3.12"
    85вҶ’
    86вҶ’[tasks]
    87вҶ’serve.run = "uvicorn app.main:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000"
    88вҶ’serve.description = "Run development server"
    89вҶ’
    90вҶ’test.run = "pytest"
    91вҶ’test.description = "Run test suite"
    92вҶ’
    93вҶ’"test:coverage".run = "pytest --cov=src --cov-report=term-missing"
    94вҶ’"test:coverage".description = "Run tests with coverage"
    95вҶ’
    96вҶ’lint.run = "ruff check ."
    97вҶ’lint.description = "Check code style"
    98вҶ’
    99вҶ’"lint:fix".run = "ruff check --fix ."
   100вҶ’format.run = "ruff format ."
   101вҶ’typecheck.run = "mypy src/"
   102вҶ’
   103вҶ’[tasks.check]
   104вҶ’description = "Full quality gate"
   105вҶ’depends = ["lint", "typecheck", "test"]
   106вҶ’
   107вҶ’[tasks."db:migrate"]
   108вҶ’description = "Run database migrations"
   109вҶ’run = "alembic upgrade head"
   110вҶ’
   111вҶ’[tasks."db:revision"]
   112вҶ’description = "Create new migration"
   113вҶ’usage = 'arg "<message>" help="Migration description"'
   114вҶ’run = "alembic revision --autogenerate -m \"$1\""
   115вҶ’
   116вҶ’[settings]
   117вҶ’experimental = true
   118вҶ’```
   119вҶ’
   120вҶ’## Rust CLI Tool
   121вҶ’
   122вҶ’Configuration for a Rust CLI project with standard Cargo workflows and source tracking.
   123вҶ’
   124вҶ’```toml
   125вҶ’[env]
   126вҶ’RUST_LOG = "info"
   127вҶ’
   128вҶ’[tools]
   129вҶ’rust = { version = "stable", components = "clippy,rustfmt" }
   130вҶ’"cargo:cargo-watch" = "latest"
   131вҶ’
   132вҶ’[tasks]
   133вҶ’build.run = "cargo build"
   134вҶ’build.description = "Build in debug mode"
   135вҶ’
   136вҶ’"build:release".run = "cargo build --release"
   137вҶ’"build:release".description = "Build optimized release binary"
   138вҶ’"build:release".sources = ["src/**/*.rs", "Cargo.toml", "Cargo.lock"]
   139вҶ’
   140вҶ’test.run = "cargo test"
   141вҶ’test.description = "Run test suite"
   142вҶ’
   143вҶ’lint.run = "cargo clippy -- -D warnings"
   144вҶ’lint.description = "Run clippy with strict warnings"
   145вҶ’
   146вҶ’format.run = "cargo fmt"
   147вҶ’"format:check".run = "cargo fmt -- --check"
   148вҶ’
   149вҶ’watch.run = "cargo watch -x build"
   150вҶ’watch.description = "Rebuild on file changes"
   151вҶ’
   152вҶ’run.run = "cargo run"
   153вҶ’run.depends = ["build"]
   154вҶ’
   155вҶ’install.run = "cargo install --path ."
   156вҶ’
   157вҶ’[tasks.check]
   158вҶ’description = "Full quality gate"
   159вҶ’depends = ["format:check", "lint", "test"]
   160вҶ’
   161вҶ’[settings]
   162вҶ’experimental = true
   163вҶ’```
   164вҶ’
   165вҶ’## Multi-Language Project
   166вҶ’
   167вҶ’Python backend with Node.js tooling (documentation site, pre-commit hooks, or frontend build).
   168вҶ’
   169вҶ’```toml
   170вҶ’[env]
   171вҶ’_.python.venv = { path = ".venv", create = true }
   172вҶ’
   173вҶ’[tools]
   174вҶ’python = "3.12"
   175вҶ’node = "22"
   176вҶ’
   177вҶ’[tasks]
   178вҶ’test.run = "pytest"
   179вҶ’lint.run = "ruff check . && eslint docs/"
   180вҶ’format.run = ["ruff format .", "prettier --write docs/"]
   181вҶ’
   182вҶ’"docs:dev".run = "npm run docs:dev"
   183вҶ’"docs:dev".description = "Run docs development server"
   184вҶ’
   185вҶ’"docs:build".run = "npm run docs:build"
   186вҶ’"docs:build".description = "Build documentation site"
   187вҶ’
   188вҶ’[tasks.check]
   189вҶ’description = "Full quality gate"
   190вҶ’depends = ["lint", "test", "docs:build"]
   191вҶ’
   192вҶ’[settings]
   193вҶ’experimental = true
   194вҶ’```
   195вҶ’
   196вҶ’## Infrastructure as Code (Terraform)
   197вҶ’
   198вҶ’Configuration for a Terraform project with environment-scoped validation, linting, and operations. Demonstrates `dir` for targeting subdirectories.
   199вҶ’
   200вҶ’```toml
   201вҶ’[settings]
   202вҶ’auto_install = true
   203вҶ’not_found_auto_install = true
   204вҶ’jobs = 4
   205вҶ’
   206вҶ’[settings.status]
   207вҶ’show_tools = true
   208вҶ’
   209вҶ’[tools]
   210вҶ’terraform = "1.14"
   211вҶ’terraform-docs = "0.21"
   212вҶ’tflint = "0.61"
   213вҶ’yamllint = "1.38"
   214вҶ’yamlfmt = "0.21"
   215вҶ’shellcheck = "0.11"
   216вҶ’
   217вҶ’[tasks.fmt]
   218вҶ’description = "Format all Terraform files recursively"
   219вҶ’dir = "infrastructure"
   220вҶ’run = "terraform fmt -recursive"
   221вҶ’
   222вҶ’[tasks.fmt-check]
   223вҶ’description = "Check formatting (CI-friendly)"
   224вҶ’dir = "infrastructure"
   225вҶ’run = "terraform fmt -recursive -check -diff"
   226вҶ’
   227вҶ’[tasks.yaml-fmt]
   228вҶ’description = "Format YAML files"
   229вҶ’run = "yamlfmt ."
   230вҶ’
   231вҶ’[tasks.prod-validate]
   232вҶ’description = "Validate production Terraform"
   233вҶ’dir = "infrastructure/environments/production"
   234вҶ’run = "terraform init -backend=false -input=false >/dev/null && terraform validate"
   235вҶ’
   236вҶ’[tasks.prod-plan]
   237вҶ’description = "Plan production changes"
   238вҶ’dir = "infrastructure/environments/production"
   239вҶ’run = "terraform plan"
   240вҶ’
   241вҶ’[tasks.prod-apply]
   242вҶ’description = "Apply production changes"
   243вҶ’dir = "infrastructure/environments/production"
   244вҶ’run = "terraform apply"
   245вҶ’
   246вҶ’[tasks.lint-prod]
   247вҶ’description = "Run TFLint in production"
   248вҶ’dir = "infrastructure/environments/production"
   249вҶ’run = "tflint --init && tflint"
   250вҶ’
   251вҶ’[tasks.shellcheck]
   252вҶ’description = "Lint shell scripts"
   253вҶ’run = "find scripts -name '*.sh' -exec shellcheck {} +"
   254вҶ’
   255вҶ’[tasks.docs]
   256вҶ’description = "Generate Terraform documentation"
   257вҶ’run = "./scripts/generate-docs.sh"
   258вҶ’
   259вҶ’[tasks.check]
   260вҶ’description = "Format, lint, and validate"
   261вҶ’depends = ["fmt", "lint-prod", "prod-validate"]
   262вҶ’env = { TF_IN_AUTOMATION = "true", TF_INPUT = "false" }
   263вҶ’
   264вҶ’[tasks.full-check]
   265вҶ’description = "Complete validation suite"
   266вҶ’depends = ["fmt", "yaml-fmt", "prod-validate", "lint-prod", "shellcheck", "docs"]
   267вҶ’env = { TF_IN_AUTOMATION = "true", TF_INPUT = "false" }
   268вҶ’
   269вҶ’[tasks.setup]
   270вҶ’description = "Initial project setup"
   271вҶ’depends = ["docs"]
   272вҶ’```
   273вҶ’
   274вҶ’## Claude Code Integration
   275вҶ’
   276вҶ’Tasks that wrap Claude Code CLI for AI-assisted workflows. Add to a project's `mise.toml` or a global `~/.config/mise/config.toml`.
   277вҶ’
   278вҶ’```toml
   279вҶ’[tasks."cc:ask"]
   280вҶ’description = "One-shot question to Claude about this project"
   281вҶ’run = "claude --print \"$1\""
   282вҶ’
   283вҶ’[tasks."cc:review"]
   284вҶ’description = "AI review of staged changes"
   285вҶ’run = "git diff --staged | claude --print 'Review these changes for bugs, style issues, and security concerns.'"
   286вҶ’
   287вҶ’[tasks."cc:explain"]
   288вҶ’description = "Explain a file using Claude"
   289вҶ’usage = 'arg "<file>" help="File to explain"'
   290вҶ’run = "cat \"$1\" | claude --print 'Explain this file. Focus on architecture and key decisions.'"
   291вҶ’
   292вҶ’[settings]
   293вҶ’experimental = true
   294вҶ’```
   295вҶ’
   296вҶ’For global cross-repo tasks, place in `~/.config/mise/config.toml`:
   297вҶ’
   298вҶ’```toml
   299вҶ’[tasks."repo:status"]
   300вҶ’description = "Show git state of all managed repos"
   301вҶ’run = '''
   302вҶ’for repo in ~/dev/projects/*/; do
   303вҶ’  if [ -d "$repo/.git" ]; then
   304вҶ’    printf "%-30s %s\n" "$(basename $repo)" "$(git -C $repo status --short | head -1)"
   305вҶ’  fi
   306вҶ’done
   307вҶ’'''
   308вҶ’```
   309вҶ’

1вҶ’---
     2вҶ’paths: plugins/**/SKILL.md, plugins/**/skills/**
     3вҶ’---
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’# Skill Development Standards
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’## SKILL.md Structure
     8вҶ’
     9вҶ’Every skill requires a `SKILL.md` file with:
    10вҶ’
    11вҶ’1. **Title** - Clear, descriptive name
    12вҶ’2. **Description** - What the skill provides
    13вҶ’3. **Triggers** - Concrete activation conditions
    14вҶ’4. **Instructions** - Step-by-step guidance
    15вҶ’5. **References** - Links to supporting files
    16вҶ’
    17вҶ’## Skill Directory Layout
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’```text
    20вҶ’skills/<skill-name>/
    21вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ SKILL.md              # Main skill definition (required)
    22вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ references/           # Reference documentation
    23вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ examples/             # Example code/configs
    24вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ patterns/             # Reusable patterns
    25вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ anti-patterns/        # Common mistakes to avoid
    26вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ tools/                # Helper scripts
    27вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җ workflows/            # Multi-step procedures
    28вҶ’```
    29вҶ’
    30вҶ’## Trigger Patterns
    31вҶ’
    32вҶ’Triggers should be **concrete and actionable**, not vague:
    33вҶ’
    34вҶ’**Good triggers:**
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’- "When creating a new Ansible role"
    37вҶ’- "When configuring Proxmox cloud-init templates"
    38вҶ’- "When writing PEP 723 inline script metadata"
    39вҶ’
    40вҶ’**Bad triggers:**
    41вҶ’
    42вҶ’- "When working with infrastructure"
    43вҶ’- "When needed"
    44вҶ’- "For Python tasks"
    45вҶ’
    46вҶ’## Reference Organization
    47вҶ’
    48вҶ’- Keep references focused and specific
    49вҶ’- Use markdown files for documentation
    50вҶ’- Include working code examples
    51вҶ’- Document anti-patterns explicitly
    52вҶ’
    53вҶ’## CRITICAL: Dynamic Bash Pattern Bug (GitHub #12762)
    54вҶ’
    55вҶ’The skill parser executes dynamic bash patterns even inside fenced code blocks.
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’**NEVER use exclamation-backtick patterns in SKILL.md code examples.** The parser executes them during skill load. Escaping with backslash does NOT work.
    58вҶ’
    59вҶ’**Use `$` shell notation instead:**
    60вҶ’
    61вҶ’```text
    62вҶ’# GOOD - Safe in code blocks:
    63вҶ’$ git status
    64вҶ’$ bash ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/script.sh
    65вҶ’```
    66вҶ’
    67вҶ’**Workarounds:**
    68вҶ’
    69вҶ’1. Use `$ command` notation in examples
    70вҶ’2. Describe syntax in prose rather than showing literal patterns
    71вҶ’3. Move examples to reference files (not parsed as skill content)
    72вҶ’
    73вҶ’This also applies to `@` file reference patterns in code blocks.
    74вҶ’

## Skill Review: mise

### Summary

The mise skill is a comprehensive guide to the mise polyglot tool version manager and task runner. It covers configuration structure, tool versions, task definitions, environment variables, layered configuration, and Claude Code integration. The skill uses progressive disclosure effectively, delegating language-specific detail and complete examples to supporting files. The description is well-constructed with strong trigger phrases. This is a high-quality skill with a few areas for tightening.

Word counts (approximate):

- SKILL.md body: ~1,950 words
- `references/python.md`: ~850 words
- `references/rust.md`: ~700 words
- `examples/examples.md`: ~1,050 words
- Total ecosystem: ~4,550 words

### Description Analysis

Current description:

> This skill provides mise configuration patterns, task definitions, and language-specific setup guidance. This skill should be used when the user asks to "set up mise", "create a mise.toml", "configure task runner", "manage tool versions", "add a task to mise", "set up Python with mise", "configure Rust toolchain", "onboard a repo", "add mise to this project", "set up dev environment", or mentions mise configuration, dev tooling setup, task definitions, or project onboarding.

Issues:

- The description is 402 characters, which sits comfortably within the recommended range.
- Uses correct third-person phrasing ("This skill should be used when...").
- Trigger phrases are concrete and specific. Good coverage of both mise-specific queries ("create a mise.toml") and intent-based queries ("onboard a repo", "set up dev environment").
- Minor gap: no trigger for environment variable configuration ("set up env vars in mise") or layered/global config queries ("configure global mise", "set up mise.local.toml").

Recommendations:

- Consider adding two more trigger phrases to close the gaps on environment and layered config use cases. Suggested improved description:

> This skill provides mise configuration patterns, task definitions, and language-specific setup guidance. This skill should be used when the user asks to "set up mise", "create a mise.toml", "configure task runner", "manage tool versions", "add a task to mise", "set up Python with mise", "configure Rust toolchain", "onboard a repo", "add mise to this project", "set up dev environment", "configure environment variables in mise", "set up global mise config", or mentions mise configuration, dev tooling setup, task definitions, layered configuration, or project onboarding.

This adds 48 characters bringing it to ~450, still under the 500-character guideline.

### Content Quality

SKILL.md Analysis:

- Word count: ~1,950 words (well within the 1,000-3,000 recommended range)
- Writing style: Predominantly imperative/infinitive. Good. Sentences like "Order sections canonically for consistency", "Specify tools in the [tools] section", "Name tasks as namespace:verb" are clean directive prose.
- Organization: Logical progression from config structure to tools to tasks to environment to layered config to integration. Sections are well-scoped and build on each other.

Issues:

1. Line 14: The opening sentence is descriptive/expository rather than imperative. "mise is a polyglot tool version manager and task runner" is fine as orientation, but the second sentence ("It manages tool versions, defines project tasks, and sets environment variables from one configuration file") is somewhat redundant with the description frontmatter. This is minor.

2. Lines 187-199, Namespace Taxonomy section: The example tasks reference specific operator tooling (`vault:triage`, `cluster:deploy`, `cc:prime`). These are useful for the operator's personal context but could confuse a general consumer of the skill. The guidance itself ("Name tasks as namespace:verb") is excellent and universal.

3. Line 264, Settings section: The `experimental = true` setting is noted as "Required for file-based tasks." This may be outdated. As of recent mise versions, file-based tasks no longer require the experimental flag. Worth verifying against current mise docs and removing if stale.

4. Lines 272-276, Claude Code Integration section: Good bridge content. The principle "mise owns the shell, Claude owns the conversation" is clear and useful. However, it references `examples/examples.md` with a relative link ("See [Examples](examples/examples.md)") -- this is fine structurally, but the actual examples file's Claude Code section (lines 274-308 of `examples/examples.md`) uses `claude --print "$1"` with double quotes, which may not work as expected in all shells when invoked through mise. This is a minor correctness concern in the examples file, not SKILL.md itself.

Recommendations:

- Verify whether `experimental = true` is still required for file-based tasks in current mise versions. If deprecated, remove the note at line 265 and the corresponding `[settings] experimental = true` lines in the examples.
- The Namespace Taxonomy section is strong. No changes needed; the operator-specific examples actually serve as good concrete illustrations even if domain-specific.

### Progressive Disclosure

Current Structure:

- `SKILL.md`: ~1,950 words (core guidance)
- `references/`: 2 files (~1,550 words total)
  - `python.md`: ~850 words
  - `rust.md`: ~700 words
- `examples/`: 1 file (~1,050 words)
  - `examples.md`: Complete mise.toml templates for 7 project types
- `scripts/`: 0 files

Assessment:

Progressive disclosure is well-implemented. The core SKILL.md covers mise fundamentals that apply regardless of language. Language-specific detail (Python venv handling, Rust cross-compilation, cargo tool extensions) is properly delegated to reference files. Complete copy-paste-ready configurations live in the examples file. The references are linked at the bottom of SKILL.md in a clear "Reference Guides" section (lines 279-282).

One structural observation: the `examples/examples.md` file includes a "Common Base Tasks" section (lines 7-59) that contains the operator's specific boilerplate (git-cliff, prek, rumdl, infisical). This is valuable for the operator's onboarding workflow but is opinionated. This is appropriate for a personal plugin marketplace and not an issue.

Recommendations:

- No major restructuring needed. The balance between core and reference content is good.
- If a Go or other language ecosystem is added in the future, the pattern of `references/<language>.md` scales naturally.

### Specific Issues

#### Critical (0)

None.

#### Major (1)

1. `/Users/basher8383/3I/workshop/lunar-claude/plugins/devops/repo-forge/skills/mise/SKILL.md` line 265: The statement "Required for file-based tasks" regarding `experimental = true` may be outdated. Recent mise versions (2024.11+) graduated file-based tasks from experimental. If this is stale, it will cause unnecessary configuration in generated files. Verify against current mise docs and update accordingly. The same setting appears in `examples/examples.md` at lines 117, 161, 192, and 292.

#### Minor (3)

1. `/Users/basher8383/3I/workshop/lunar-claude/plugins/devops/repo-forge/skills/mise/SKILL.md` lines 279-282: The reference links section lists "Examples" twice in slightly different ways (as a bullet item and implicitly through the section heading). The third bullet duplicates info from the second reference link. Consider whether both the "Reference Guides" bullets and the "External Resources" section are both needed, or if the reference guides section could be streamlined to just the two language references since examples are already mentioned in the Claude Code Integration section above it.

2. `/Users/basher8383/3I/workshop/lunar-claude/plugins/devops/repo-forge/skills/mise/examples/examples.md` lines 279-293: The `cc:ask` task uses `"$1"` for argument passing. This works but the task lacks a `usage` declaration for the argument, unlike the `cc:explain` task which has one. Inconsistent argument handling patterns within the same file.

3. `/Users/basher8383/3I/workshop/lunar-claude/plugins/devops/repo-forge/skills/mise/SKILL.md` description frontmatter: Missing `version` field. While optional, including a version number helps track skill evolution, especially in a marketplace context where plugins already carry version metadata.

### Positive Aspects

- The description frontmatter is one of the strongest I have reviewed. It uses correct third-person phrasing, includes 10+ concrete trigger phrases spanning both tool-specific and intent-based queries, and stays within length guidelines.
- Progressive disclosure is textbook. Core concepts in SKILL.md, language-specific depth in references, complete templates in examples. The skill loads fast with focused content and offers drill-down paths.
- The Namespace Taxonomy section (lines 187-201) codifies a naming convention that prevents the common "flat task name soup" problem. The `namespace:verb` pattern with concrete rationale is immediately actionable.
- The Layered Configuration section (lines 219-244) addresses a real-world pain point (environment switching) with a practical pattern using `mise.local.toml` generation.
- The Claude Code Integration section (lines 272-276) establishes a clean boundary principle ("mise owns the shell, Claude owns the conversation") that prevents anti-patterns like reimplementing slash commands in shell scripts.
- Writing style is consistently imperative throughout. No second-person drift, no passive voice accumulation.

### Overall Rating

Pass

This is a well-structured, well-written skill that follows best practices for description triggers, progressive disclosure, and content organization. The single major issue (potentially stale `experimental = true` guidance) is a factual accuracy concern rather than a structural one. The minor issues are polish-level.

### Priority Recommendations

1. Verify whether `experimental = true` is still required for file-based tasks in current mise versions. If it has graduated from experimental, update SKILL.md line 265 and remove the setting from all four occurrences in `examples/examples.md`.

2. Add trigger phrases for environment variable configuration and layered/global config scenarios to the description frontmatter, as suggested above.

3. Add a `usage` declaration to the `cc:ask` task in `examples/examples.md` to match the pattern used by `cc:explain`, ensuring consistent argument handling across examples.

## Settings

```toml
[settings]
experimental = true                 # Required for file-based tasks
jobs = 4                            # Parallel task execution
task_output = "prefix"              # prefix | interleave | quiet | silent
```

Language-specific settings nest under the tool name: `python.compile`, `rust.cargo_home`. See language reference files for details.

## Claude Code Integration

mise tasks orchestrate shell-side operations around Claude Code sessions. The pattern: mise owns the shell, Claude owns the conversation. When a mise task needs Claude, invoke the CLI with `claude --print`. Reserve interactive `claude` for workflows requiring user interaction. Never reimplement slash command logic in mise вҖ” wrap it.

description = "Initial project setup"
depends = ["docs"]
```

## Claude Code Integration

Tasks that wrap Claude Code CLI for AI-assisted workflows. Add to a project's `mise.toml` or a global `~/.config/mise/config.toml`.

```toml
[tasks."cc:ask"]
description = "One-shot question to Claude about this project"
run = "claude --print \"$1\""

[tasks."cc:review"]
description = "AI review of staged changes"
run = "git diff --staged | claude --print 'Review these changes for bugs, style issues, and security concerns.'"

[tasks."cc:explain"]
description = "Explain a file using Claude"
usage = 'arg "<file>" help="File to explain"'
run = "cat \"$1\" | claude --print 'Explain this file. Focus on architecture and key decisions.'"

[settings]
experimental = true
```

For global cross-repo tasks, place in `~/.config/mise/config.toml`:

```toml
[tasks."repo:status"]
description = "Show git state of all managed repos"
run = '''
for repo in ~/dev/projects/*/; do
  if [ -d "$repo/.git" ]; then
    printf "%-30s %s\n" "$(basename $repo)" "$(git -C $repo status --short | head -1)"
  fi
done
'''
```

# mise

mise is a polyglot tool version manager and task runner. A single `mise.toml` replaces Makefiles, shell scripts, and language-specific version managers (nvm, pyenv, rustup). It manages tool versions, defines project tasks, and sets environment variables from one configuration file.

## Config File Structure

Order sections canonically for consistency:

```toml
min_version = "2024.9.5"

[env]
DATABASE_URL = "postgres://localhost/myapp_dev"

[tools]
python = "3.12"

[tasks]
test.run = "pytest"

[settings]
auto_install = true
not_found_auto_install = true
task_run_auto_install = true
```

Use `min_version` to enforce a minimum mise version. Recognized top-level sections: `[env]`, `[tools]`, `[tasks]`, `[settings]`, `[plugins]`, `[tool_alias]`, `[task_config]`, `[vars]`.

## Tool Version Management

Specify tools in the `[tools]` section. mise installs and activates them automatically.

```toml
[tools]
node = "22"                   # Major version prefix
python = "3.12.2"             # Exact version
go = "latest"                 # Latest stable
erlang = "lts"                # LTS channel
```

Multiple versions install side-by-side (first is default):

```toml
[tools]
python = ["3.11", "3.12"]
```

### Version formats

| Format | Example | Behavior |
|--------|---------|----------|
| Exact | `"3.12.2"` | Specific version |
| Prefix | `"3.12"` | Latest matching 3.12.x |
| Channel | `"latest"`, `"lts"` | Resolved at install time |
| Reference | `"ref:main"` | Git ref, compiled from source |
| Subtraction | `"sub-1:latest"` | One major behind latest |

### Table form with options

```toml
[tools]
node = { version = "22", postinstall = "corepack enable" }
rust = { version = "stable", components = "clippy,rustfmt", profile = "default" }
```

### Backend syntax for non-core tools

```toml
[tools]
"ubi:astral-sh/ruff" = "latest"     # GitHub binary releases
"cargo:cargo-watch" = "latest"       # Cargo crate
"npm:prettier" = "3"                 # npm package
"pipx:ansible-lint" = "latest"       # Python CLI tools via pipx
```

### Custom plugins

Register custom tool plugins in `[plugins]`:

```toml
[plugins]
fnox-env = "https://github.com/jdx/mise-env-fnox"
```

## Task Definitions

Tasks are defined in `[tasks]` or as executable files in `.mise/tasks/`.

### Inline tasks

```toml
tasks.test = "pytest"
tasks.lint = "ruff check ."
tasks.format = "ruff format ."
```

### Table tasks

```toml
[tasks.build]
description = "Build the project"
depends = ["lint"]
run = "cargo build --release"
dir = "{{config_root}}/backend"
env = { RUST_LOG = "info" }
sources = ["src/**/*.rs", "Cargo.toml"]
outputs = ["target/release/myapp"]
```

### Task-level options

Pin tool versions per-task, set aliases for shortcuts, or override the shell:

```toml
[tasks."lint:shellcheck"]
description = "Lint shell scripts"
run = "shellcheck scripts/*.sh"
tools = { "shellcheck" = "0.11.0" }    # Pin tool version for this task
alias = "sc"                            # Short alias for mise run sc
shell = "bash -c"                       # Override default shell
```

### Task directory scoping

Use `dir` to run a task in a specific subdirectory. Essential for monorepos and infrastructure projects where different tasks target different paths:

```toml
[tasks.prod-validate]
description = "Validate production Terraform"
dir = "infrastructure/environments/production"
run = "terraform init -backend=false -input=false >/dev/null && terraform validate"

[tasks.prod-plan]
description = "Plan production changes"
dir = "infrastructure/environments/production"
run = "terraform plan"
```

Relative paths resolve from the config file's directory. Use `{{config_root}}` for explicit anchoring. When multiple tasks share a base directory, the pattern of environment-scoped tasks (same command, different `dir`) keeps configs DRY while remaining explicit about scope.

### Multi-line scripts

```toml
[tasks.setup]
run = [
  "pip install -e '.[dev]'",
  "pre-commit install"
]
```

### File-based tasks

Place executable scripts in `.mise/tasks/`. Subdirectories create namespaces вҖ” `.mise/tasks/db/migrate` becomes task `db:migrate`.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#MISE description="Run database migrations"
#MISE depends=["db:check"]

alembic upgrade head
```

Mark file tasks executable (`chmod +x`). Any language works via shebang.

### Task arguments

```toml
[tasks.deploy]
usage = '''
arg "<environment>" help="Target environment" default="staging"
flag "-v --verbose" help="Verbose output"
'''
run = "deploy.sh ${usage_environment}"
```

## Namespace Taxonomy

Name tasks as `namespace:verb`. The namespace is a domain noun, the verb is an action. This groups related tasks alphabetically in `mise tasks` output.

```toml
# Per-project namespaces use the project's domain
tasks."vault:triage" = "claude --print /inbox-process"
tasks."cluster:deploy" = "talosctl apply-config"

# Cross-project namespaces for shared concerns
tasks."cc:prime" = "claude --print /vault-prime"
tasks."git:clean" = "git branch --merged | grep -v main | xargs git branch -d"
```

Use the project's primary domain noun as the namespace. Avoid generic names like `run:` or `do:`.

## Environment Variables

```toml
[env]
NODE_ENV = "development"
PROJECT_ROOT = "{{config_root}}"

# Load from dotenv
_.file = ".env"

# Python virtual environment
_.python.venv = { path = ".venv", create = true }
```

Template variables: `{{config_root}}` (directory containing mise.toml), `{{env.VAR}}` (existing env vars).

## Layered Configuration

mise resolves configuration from multiple levels, with closer files taking precedence:

```text
~/.config/mise/config.toml       # Global: cross-cutting tools and tasks
./mise.toml                      # Project: local tools and tasks
./mise.local.toml                # Local overrides (gitignored)
```

Global config handles cross-cutting concerns (shared tools, repo orchestration tasks). Project config handles repo-specific tools and tasks. Global tasks never assume a specific repo structure вҖ” use environment variables or auto-detection.

Use `mise.local.toml` (gitignored) for environment-specific overrides. Tasks can generate this file to switch environments:

```toml
[tasks."env:local"]
description = "Switch to local LAN environment"
run = '''
cat > .mise.local.toml << 'EOF'
[env]
NOMAD_ADDR = "http://192.168.11.11:4646"
CONSUL_HTTP_ADDR = "http://192.168.11.11:8500"
EOF
echo "Switched to local. Run 'mise trust' to apply."
'''
```

## Task Dependencies

```toml
[tasks.check]
description = "Full quality gate"
depends = ["format:check", "lint", "test"]

[tasks.deploy]
depends = ["check"]
depends_post = ["notify"]           # Runs after this task completes
wait_for = ["build"]                # Waits without forcing execution
```

Independent dependencies run in parallel by default. Control parallelism with `[settings] jobs = 4`.

## Settings

```toml
[settings]
experimental = true                 # Required for file-based tasks
jobs = 4                            # Parallel task execution
task_output = "prefix"              # prefix | interleave | quiet | silent
```

Language-specific settings nest under the tool name: `python.compile`, `rust.cargo_home`. See language reference files for details.

## Claude Code Integration

mise tasks orchestrate shell-side operations around Claude Code sessions. The pattern: mise owns the shell, Claude owns the conversation. When a mise task needs Claude, invoke the CLI with `claude --print`. Reserve interactive `claude` for workflows requiring user interaction. Never reimplement slash command logic in mise вҖ” wrap it.

See [Examples](examples/examples.md) for complete Claude Code integration task patterns.

## Reference Guides

- [Python Ecosystem](references/python.md) вҖ” Version management, venv handling, and task patterns for Python projects
- [Rust Ecosystem](references/rust.md) вҖ” Toolchain management and task patterns for Rust projects
- [Examples](examples/examples.md) вҖ” Complete `mise.toml` files for common project types

## External Resources

- [mise documentation](https://mise.jdx.dev/) вҖ” Official docs
- [mise GitHub](https://github.com/jdx/mise) вҖ” Source repository
- [mise.toml schema](https://mise.jdx.dev/schema/mise.json) вҖ” JSON schema for editor validation

## Definition

According to the documentation, the `experimental` setting "Enables experimental features. I generally will publish new features under this config which needs to be enabled to use them."

The key points are:

- **Type**: boolean
- **Environment variable**: `MISE_EXPERIMENTAL`
- **Default**: `false`

## Features Requiring experimental = true

The documentation states: "While a feature is marked as 'experimental' its behavior may change or even disappear in any release."

However, **the documentation does not specify which particular features currently require this setting**. It only explains that experimental features may be iterated on to "get the behavior right" before the experimental label is removed.

## File-Based Tasks

There is **no mention** in this settings documentation of file-based tasks requiring the experimental flag. The task-related settings discussed (under the `task` section) do not reference an experimental requirement.
